sections.heading: Adenocarcinoma/Carcinoma NOS: Predominant and Isolated Cervical Nodes
sections.content:
Adenocarcinoma or carcinoma not otherwise specified (Carcinoma NOS) with predominant and isolated cervical node metastases.
Options:
- Follow workup for isolated cervical nodes (Mandatory): Perform neck/chest/abdomen/pelvis CT if not done. Consider endoscopy if clinically indicated. Perform appropriate IHC stains. Treat per NCCN Guidelines for Head and Neck Cancers if primary is found.
- Treat as per Head and Neck Cancers (Preferred): If a head and neck primary is identified or suspected, manage according to the NCCN Guidelines for Head and Neck Cancers.

sections.heading: Adenocarcinoma/Carcinoma NOS: Supraclavicular Nodes
sections.content:
Adenocarcinoma/Carcinoma NOS with supraclavicular node involvement.
Options:
- Additional Workup (Mandatory): Perform neck/chest/abdomen/pelvis CT if not done. Consider endoscopy if clinically indicated. Perform appropriate IHC. Consider mammogram (intact breast tissue) and PSA (>40 y with prostate). Treat per NCCN disease-specific guidelines if primary found.
- Locoregional/Systemic therapy (Other recommended): Consider radiation therapy (RT) to the nodal basin if clinically indicated. Systemic therapy may be considered based on clinical scenario and patient factors.

sections.heading: Adenocarcinoma/Carcinoma NOS: Axillary Nodes
sections.content:
Adenocarcinoma/Carcinoma NOS with axillary node involvement.
Options:
- Breast-focused workup (Mandatory): Perform mammogram (in those with intact breast tissue including gynecomastia). If nondiagnostic and histopathologic evidence for breast cancer, breast MRI and/or breast ultrasound indicated. Perform appropriate IHC. Consider PSA (>40 y with prostate).
- Treat per Breast Cancer Guidelines (Preferred): If breast cancer is confirmed or highly suspected, treat per NCCN Guidelines for Breast Cancer Screening and Diagnosis and NCCN Guidelines for Breast Cancer.

sections.heading: Adenocarcinoma/Carcinoma NOS: Mediastinum
sections.content:
Adenocarcinoma/Carcinoma NOS with mediastinal involvement.
Options:
- Age-stratified workup (Mandatory): Perform chest/abdomen/pelvis CT if not done. Test beta-hCG and alpha-fetoprotein. Consider testicular ultrasound if markers elevated (in those with testes, <50 y). Perform appropriate IHC. Consider mammogram and PSA.
- Age-based treatment decision (Preferred): <40 y: Treat as poor-risk germ cell tumor per NCCN Guidelines for Testicular Cancer or Ovarian Cancer. 40 to <50 y: Treat as poor-risk germ cell tumor OR per NCCN Guidelines for Non-Small Cell Lung Cancer. ≥50 y: Treat per NCCN Guidelines for Non-Small Cell Lung Cancer.
